world.title: Just like Iraq, Rupert Murdoch's fingerprints are all over Iran
world.description: Rupert Murdoch and News Corp are continuing their sycophantic support of US warmongering, this time in Iran.

world.title: Trump team eyes Iran speaker Ghalibaf as possible partner, future leader: Politico
world.description: Washington quietly explores Iran insider Ghalibaf for diplomacy amid conflict

world.title: Military plane with 121 people crashes in southwestern Colombia
world.description: Colombian officials say that a military cargo plane with 121 people, most of them soldiers, crashed shortly after taking off in southwestern Colombia.

world.title: Americas: States intensify crackdown on civil society through “anti-NGO laws”
world.description: New Amnesty report: Countries across Americas are adopting restrictive legal frameworks to weaken and control civil society organizations.

world.title: A&K Sanctuary unveils Kitirua Plains Lodge
world.description: A&K Sanctuary has unveiled Kitirua Plains Lodge, a striking new property in Kenya's Amboseli region that represents a bold departure from traditional safari architecture.

world.title: EU-Mercosur trade deal to apply provisionally from May, despite pending court ruling on legality
world.description: Brussels has ploughed ahead as it pushes to diversify trade in the face of challenges from the United States and China.

world.title: Blow to Italian PM Giorgia Meloni as she suffers referendum defeat
world.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni concedes defeat in a referendum on justice reform, but despite the major blow to her far-right leadership, she insists she is going nowhere.

world.title: Iran denies any talks with US after Trump claims ‘productive’ discussions
world.description: Iran parliament speaker says US president using idea of talks to ‘escape quagmire in which US and Israel are trapped’.

business.title: The FCC Just Banned the Sale of New Wi-Fi Router Models Made Outside US
business.description: The surprising order means any new Wi-Fi router models sold in the country must be US-made, or receive an exemption from the Pentagon or Homeland Security Department.

business.title: Palo Alto Networks Secures Agentic AI with Prisma AIRS 3.0
business.description: /PRNewswire/ -- Palo Alto Networks (NASDAQ: PANW), the global cybersecurity leader, today advances its AI security platform with the launch of Prisma® AIRS™...

business.title: US panel credits China’s AI edge to open-source models, manufacturing dominance
business.description: American Congressional commission concludes China’s open ecosystem has seen performance gaps narrowed with top Western large language models.

business.title: Microsoft Hires Ali Farhadi to Work With Mustafa Suleyman on AI
business.description: Microsoft hired Ali Farhadi, former Allen Institute CEO, to join Mustafa Suleyman's AI team as corporate VP.

business.title: Passengers Prepared for a Rough Landing Before Crash at LaGuardia
business.description: A flight attendant was ejected from the airplane, a passenger said, but the people onboard still managed to open an emergency door and evacuate themselves from the plane.

business.title: New push to drill for oil off Otago coast
business.description: A newly-formed company is launching a fresh bid to find oil and gas off the coast of Otago. CBX Energy Limited, which was incorporated as a company...

business.title: Innovative Food Holdings, Inc. Announces Updated Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2025 Earnings Call
business.description: BROADVIEW, Ill., March 23, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Innovative Food Holdings, Inc. (OTCQB: IVFH) ('IVFH” or the 'Company”), a national seller of gourmet specialty foods, today announced an updated schedule for its conference call to discuss fourth quarter and fiscal year 2025 financial results.

business.title: Gas prices expected to drop in Toronto by 10 to 11 cents
business.description: Gas prices are expected to tank by 10 to 11 cents on Wednesday, according to an early outlook from price specialists at En-Pro.

science.title: New findings on the first steps in protein synthesis
science.description: In the earliest phase of creating human proteins, the protein complex NAC performs an essential task by starting the first steps toward folding proteins into their correct three-dimensional structures. An international research team led by scientists from the Center for Molecular Biology of Heidelberg University has found that the NAC complex binds to the nascent protein building blocks formed by the ribosome, i.e., the amino acid chains, and initiates the folding that is essential for functional proteins.

science.title: A Deep-Sea Expedition Reveals a Hidden Underwater World in a Deep Mediterranean Canyon, Untouched for Centuries
science.description: A newly discovered world hidden deep beneath the waters off Naples reveals an untouched ecosystem that could reshape marine conservation.

science.title: Single-cell sequencing reveals unexpected protist diversity
science.description: Researchers from the Earlham Institute, in collaboration with the Department of Biology at the University of Oxford, have discovered three previously unrecognized lineages of the protist Bodo, each with its own bacterial endosymbiont (a symbiotic organism living within the body of its host).

science.title: Mollusks pushed evolutionary limits before becoming predictable
science.description: Researchers find that mollusk evolution grew more predictable after millions of years of evolutionary change.

science.title: Chaos as a matter of direction: Researchers build layered material where order and disorder coexist
science.description: Some materials behave unexpectedly. They crack differently than expected, or react in ways that are hard to explain. The answer often lies in their atomic structure. Is it neatly arranged, as in a crystal, or disordered, as in glass? Researchers at the University of Twente have now created a material that is both simultaneously. In two directions it is disordered; in the third, perfectly ordered. Their findings have been published in Nature Communications.

science.title: From slices to whole bodies: How 3D cell atlases could reshape pathology research
science.description: In conventional pathology and physiology research, two-dimensional (2D) analysis—observing thinly sliced tissue sections—has been mainstream, making it difficult to comprehensively understand the distribution of cells across entire organs or throughout the whole organism. Therefore, a new analytical platform has been needed to quantitatively elucidate developmental processes of the organism, drug efficacy and adverse effects, and disease-related changes at the whole-organ or whole-body level.

science.title: Rubin Alert Leads to First Follow-Up Observations and Detection of Four Supernovae
science.description: NSF NOIRLab has completed end-to-end runs of its ecosystem for following up on alerts from NSF–DOE Vera C. Rubin Observatory. The runs demonstrated how multiple NOIRLab-developed software tools, plus a network of telescopes around the globe, will enable quick follow-up observations of the countless transient objects that Rubin will uncover during its ten-year survey.

science.title: Study reshapes understanding of interaction between organelles in animal cells
science.description: Findings from a new University of Cincinnati study have reshaped the fundamental understanding of how a certain cell organelle prepares its environment for cellular digestion. The study, led by UC's Jiajie Diao, Ph.D. and published in Cell Reports, focused on the lysosome, an organelle within animal cells that contributes to a number of cellular functions. This includes helping to break down materials into more useful energy sources, similar to how the intestines digest food into usable nutrients on the whole-body level.

ai.title: US panel credits China’s AI edge to open-source models, manufacturing dominance
ai.description: American Congressional commission concludes China’s open ecosystem has seen performance gaps narrowed with top Western large language models.

ai.title: Vaishnaw launches initiatives aimed at boosting media and entertainment sector
ai.description: NEW DELHI, Mar 23 : Information and Broadcasting Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw on Monday launched three major initiatives aimed at strengthening India’s media and entertainment sector and promoting the creative economy. The three measures are: National AI Skilling initiative in partnership with Google and YouTube through the Indian Institute of Creative Technologies (IICT); MyWAVES, a citizen creator platform on WAVES OTT; and the rollout of Advanced Electronic Programme Guide (EPG) and in-built satellite tuners in television sets for enhancing access to […]

ai.title: MP News: State AI Mission To Be Launched Shortly, Says CM Mohan Yadav
ai.description: Chief Minister Mohan Yadav announced that the State AI Mission will be launched soon to strengthen governance, enhance transparency, and enable early risk detection in sectors like agriculture, health, nutrition, and disaster management. The initiative will be implemented in phases from 2026-27, integrating AI technologies and developing permanent institutional capacity.

ai.title: A man used AI to help make a cancer vaccine for his dog-an oncologist urges caution
ai.description: An Australian tech entrepreneur has helped create what appears to be a made-to-measure cancer vaccine for his dog, Rosie, using artificial intelligence tools such as ChatGPT as part of the process.

ai.title: Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak says he is not a fan of AI
ai.description: Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak warns that growing dependence on AI could change how people think, arguing rapid innovation is outpacing the technology's reliability.

ai.title: Stock Market Rallies As Trump Flags 'Productive' Iran Talks; AI Name Gains
ai.description: The Dow Jones Industrial Average and other indexes rise in Monday's stock market after Trump signals 'productive' discussions with Iran.

ai.title: Obsidian proves that note-taking doesn't need AI to be powerful
This Android brand is launching the first true OpenClaw-powered AI agent on a phone
ai.description: Android phone brand TECNO will soon integrate OpenClaw to bring agentic features to its Android smartphones.
